summary: twokey postinstall silently downloads and executes an unpinned GitHub 'latest' AppImage with persistent autostart
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook (bin/postinstall.js) silently spawns bin/twokey.js with `--desktop --enable-autostart --quiet`. That script queries https://api.github.com/repos/meinzeug/twokey/releases/latest, downloads the first.AppImage asset to ~/.local/share/twokey/bin/twokey-ai.AppImage with mode 0o755, and spawns it detached — no user prompt, no hash/signature verification, and no README disclosure. The release pointer is mutable ('latest'), so any future release published by (or as) the maintainer is fetched and executed on every reinstall. The same flow writes a systemd user unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/twokey.service and runs `systemctl --user enable --now twokey.service`, ensuring the downloaded binary launches on every login. Although the publisher matches the package, fetching unpinned and unverified executable bytes from a mutable 'latest' pointer at install time, plus silently installing autostart persistence, is the canonical install-time-rce/dropper shape: a future credential or account compromise would deliver arbitrary native code to every installer with no signal.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
